# AtomicMemory

> Standardized platform layer for AI memory, pluggable at every seam.

## Get started

- [Introduction](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/index.md): AtomicMemory is an open-source memory engine for AI applications, semantic retrieval, AUDN mutation (Add / Update / Delete / No-op), and contradiction-safe claim versioning, delivered as an HTTP service you can run from a published Docker image.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/quickstart.md): Start a local memory service from the published Docker image, then run your

## Platform

- [Architecture](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/platform/architecture.md): AtomicMemory is a standardized platform layer for AI memory, not a monolithic service.
- [Artifact Storage](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/platform/artifact-storage.md): Artifact storage is the optional raw-byte layer behind documents and storage
- [Composition](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/platform/composition.md): AtomicMemory has an explicit composition root, a single function, createCoreRuntime, that owns every piece of wiring between config, the Postgres pool, repositories, stores, and services.
- [Consuming core](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/platform/consuming-core.md): atomicmemory-core can be used as a network service, embedded in a Node process, or booted as a containerized release target.
- [Lessons](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/platform/lessons.md): A lesson is a recorded failure pattern that AtomicMemory has seen for a
- [Observability](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/platform/observability.md): Every AtomicMemory retrieval emits a structured, stable observability
- [Providers](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/platform/providers.md): Embeddings and LLM calls in AtomicMemory are pluggable providers behind
- [Scope](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/platform/scope.md): MemoryScope is AtomicMemory's canonical read-path contract: a single tagged
- [Stores](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/platform/stores.md): AtomicMemory's storage layer is exposed as a family of narrow, domain-facing

## SDK

- [Errors](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/api/errors.md): Every error the SDK throws descends from AtomicMemoryError.
- [MemoryProvider contract](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/api/memory-provider.md): This is the authoring contract for anyone implementing a MemoryProvider.
- [Reference overview](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/api/overview.md): The SDK's reference documentation comes in two shapes: hand-written contract pages (here, under API Reference) and generated type / method docs produced from the SDK's source.
- [Architecture](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/concepts/architecture.md): MemoryClient is a layered dispatcher.
- [Capabilities](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/concepts/capabilities.md): Not every memory backend supports every operation.
- [Embeddings](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/concepts/embeddings.md): The SDK ships local embedding generation through @huggingface/transformers.
- [Memory providers](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/concepts/provider-model.md): The provider model is what makes MemoryClient backend-agnostic.
- [Scopes and identity](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/concepts/scopes-and-identity.md): Every SDK operation carries a scope, the partition the memory lives in.
- [Storage adapters](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/concepts/storage-adapters.md): This page describes the SDK's client-side key/value storage adapters.
- [Artifact storage](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/guides/artifact-storage.md): The SDKs expose core's artifact-storage API for applications that need to
- [Using the atomicmemory backend](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/guides/atomicmemory-backend.md): AtomicMemoryProvider is the SDK's HTTP client for atomicmemory-core.
- [Browser entry](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/guides/browser-helpers.md): The SDK ships a slim entry point for applications that run in the browser against a backend-hosted memory engine.
- [Browser primitives](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/guides/browser-primitives.md): This guide bypasses MemoryClient.
- [Writing a custom provider](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/guides/custom-provider.md): The provider interface is deliberately small.
- [Using the Mem0 backend](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/guides/mem0-backend.md): Mem0Provider is the SDK's HTTP client for Mem0.
- [Node.js / server-side](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/guides/nodejs-server.md): Using the SDK server-side is supported, the package is ESM + CJS, has no browser-only code on its critical path, and the subpath storage primitives run anywhere Node does.
- [Swapping backends](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/guides/swapping-backends.md): The SDK's provider architecture makes the capability of swapping backends real; this guide covers the operational story.
- [SDK Overview](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/overview.md): A TypeScript client for memory and artifact storage, pluggable across backends.
- [SDK Quickstart](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/sdk/quickstart.md): Install, initialize, and make your first ingest and search, with MemoryClient wired to a running atomicmemory-core.

## CLI

- [AtomicMemory CLI](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/cli.md): atomicmemory is the human- and agent-facing command line for AtomicMemory.

## Integrations

- [Claude Code](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/claude-code.md): The Claude Code plugin is discovered from the public AtomicMemory monorepo.
- [Claude Code Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/claude-code/cloud.md): Cloud deployment details for Claude Code are coming soon.
- [Claude Code Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/claude-code/local.md): Give Claude Code persistent, cross-session memory backed by AtomicMemory.
- [Codex](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/codex.md): AtomicMemory integrates with Codex through the shared MCP server and a memory protocol skill that handles retrieval, durable writes, and handoff snapshots.
- [Codex Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/codex/cloud.md): Cloud deployment details for Codex are coming soon.
- [Codex Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/codex/local.md): Give Codex persistent, cross-session memory backed by AtomicMemory.
- [Cursor](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/cursor.md): AtomicMemory supports Cursor today through a local MCP integration and Cursor
- [Cursor Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/cursor/cloud.md): Cursor Cloud deployment details are coming soon.
- [Cursor Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/cursor/local.md): Cursor support is available today as a manual local integration using the
- [Hermes Agent](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/hermes.md): AtomicMemory integrates with Hermes as a native Python memory provider.
- [Hermes Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/hermes/cloud.md): Cloud deployment details for Hermes are coming soon.
- [Hermes Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/hermes/local.md): Give Hermes Agent persistent, cross-session memory backed by AtomicMemory.
- [OpenClaw](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/openclaw.md): AtomicMemory integrates with OpenClaw as a plugin and skill bundle backed by the same MCP server and SDK primitives used elsewhere in the platform.
- [OpenClaw Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/openclaw/cloud.md): Cloud deployment details for OpenClaw are coming soon.
- [OpenClaw Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/coding-agents/openclaw/local.md): Give OpenClaw persistent, cross-channel memory backed by AtomicMemory.
- [LangChain (JS)](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/langchain-js.md): AtomicMemory provides LangChain JS helpers and agent tools around an injected AtomicMemory SDK client.
- [LangChain (JS) Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/langchain-js/cloud.md): Cloud deployment details for the LangChain adapter are coming soon.
- [LangChain (JS) Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/langchain-js/local.md): AtomicMemory support for LangChain JS exposes durable semantic memory through helper functions and LangChain-native tools.
- [LangGraph (JS)](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/langgraph-js.md): AtomicMemory provides LangGraph node factories that add durable retrieval and ingest beside graph-scoped checkpointers.
- [LangGraph (JS) Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/langgraph-js/cloud.md): Cloud deployment details for the LangGraph adapter are coming soon.
- [LangGraph (JS) Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/langgraph-js/local.md): AtomicMemory support for LangGraph JS provides durable semantic memory next to LangGraph's run-scoped checkpointers.
- [Mastra](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/mastra.md): AtomicMemory provides Mastra tools that map agent memory search and ingest onto AtomicMemory retrieval and ingest primitives.
- [Mastra Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/mastra/cloud.md): Cloud deployment details for the Mastra adapter are coming soon.
- [Mastra Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/mastra/local.md): AtomicMemory support for Mastra exposes durable memory search and ingest as Mastra tools around your SDK MemoryClient.
- [OpenAI Agents SDK](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/openai-agents.md): AtomicMemory integrates with the OpenAI Agents SDK through an adapter that augments run input with memory and ingests completed turns back into durable storage.
- [OpenAI Agents SDK Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/openai-agents/cloud.md): Cloud deployment details for the OpenAI Agents SDK adapter are coming soon.
- [OpenAI Agents SDK Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/openai-agents/local.md): Give OpenAI Agents SDK applications persistent memory backed by AtomicMemory.
- [Vercel AI SDK](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/vercel-ai-sdk.md): AtomicMemory integrates with Vercel AI SDK through an adapter that handles retrieval before the model call and durable ingest after the turn completes.
- [Vercel AI SDK Cloud](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/vercel-ai-sdk/cloud.md): Cloud deployment details for the Vercel AI SDK adapter are coming soon.
- [Vercel AI SDK Local](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/frameworks/vercel-ai-sdk/local.md): Give Vercel AI SDK applications persistent memory backed by AtomicMemory.
- [Integrations](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/integrations/overview.md): AtomicMemory adds durable memory to coding agents, AI frameworks, and terminal workflows through the same provider-backed memory layer.

## API Reference

- [AtomicMemory HTTP API](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/atomicmemory-http-api.md): Semantic memory engine for AI applications.
- [Auto-resolve all expired conflicts for a user.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/auto-resolve-agent-conflicts.md): Auto-resolve all expired conflicts for a user.
- [Memory-cap status for a user's store.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/check-memory-cap.md): Memory-cap status for a user's store.
- [Compute consolidation candidates; optionally execute (execute=true).](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/consolidate-memories.md): Compute consolidation candidates; optionally execute (execute=true).
- [Conventions](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/conventions.md): General conventions that apply across every /v1/memories/ and /v1/agents/ route.
- [Deactivate a lesson by id.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/deactivate-lesson.md): Deactivate a lesson by id.
- [Delete one allowed disposable test scope.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/delete-admin-scope.md): Mounted only when CORE_ADMIN_API_KEY and CORE_TEST_SCOPE_ALLOW_PATTERN are both configured.
- [Soft-delete (tombstone) a document.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/delete-document.md): Idempotent: a second DELETE on the same id returns success with `already_deleted: true`.
- [Delete a single memory by UUID.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/delete-memory.md): Delete a single memory by UUID.
- [Soft-delete an artifact (optionally cascading documents).](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/delete-storage-artifact.md): Reference-aware delete.
- [Evaluate decay candidates. dry_run=false archives them.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/evaluate-decay.md): Evaluate decay candidates.
- [Expand a list of memory IDs into full objects.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/expand-memories.md): Expand a list of memory IDs into full objects.
- [Look up the trust level for a (user, agent) pair.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-agent-trust.md): Look up the trust level for a (user, agent) pair.
- [Aggregate mutation statistics for a user's memory store.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-audit-summary.md): Aggregate mutation statistics for a user's memory store.
- [Fetch a single document by UUID.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-document.md): Fetch a single document by UUID.
- [Read upload + index byte caps and raw-storage capability.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-document-limits.md): Public preflight surface.
- [Lesson statistics for a user.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-lesson-stats.md): Lesson statistics for a user.
- [Fetch a single memory by UUID.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-memory.md): Fetch a single memory by UUID.
- [Per-memory version history.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-memory-audit-trail.md): Per-memory version history.
- [Subsystem liveness + current runtime config snapshot.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-memory-health.md): Subsystem liveness + current runtime config snapshot.
- [Recent mutations for a user, limit-bounded.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-recent-audit.md): Recent mutations for a user, limit-bounded.
- [Get deferred-mutation reconciliation status.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-reconcile-status.md): Get deferred-mutation reconciliation status.
- [Aggregate memory statistics for a user.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-stats.md): Aggregate memory statistics for a user.
- [Read an artifact metadata projection.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-storage-artifact.md): Read an artifact metadata projection.
- [Read the raw bytes for a managed artifact.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-storage-artifact-content.md): Returns the artifact bytes for managed-mode artifacts.
- [Read the direct storage API capability snapshot.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/get-storage-capabilities.md): Public preflight surface for the storage API.
- [Chunk + embed text for a registered document.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/index-document.md): Deterministic char-window chunking, batched embeddings via the core embedding provider, and one provenance-linked memory per chunk.
- [Ingest a conversation transcript with full extraction.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/ingest-memory.md): Full-extraction ingest.
- [Quick ingest (storeVerbatim when skip_extraction=true).](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/ingest-memory-quick.md): Quick or verbatim ingest.
- [List open agent conflicts for a user.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/list-agent-conflicts.md): List open agent conflicts for a user.
- [List active documents for a user, optionally filtered by source_site.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/list-documents.md): List active documents for a user, optionally filtered by source_site.
- [Cursor-paginated user-scoped document list with status-bucket filter.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/list-documents-for-user.md): Returns active documents for the supplied `user_id`, ordered `(created_at DESC, id DESC)`.
- [Documents WITHOUT non-deleted memories, narrowed by recovery-status filter.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/list-documents-without-memories.md): Backs the passport synthetic-row stream and the UI 'uploaded but unindexed' surface.
- [List active lessons for a user.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/list-lessons.md): List active lessons for a user.
- [List memories for a user (or workspace).](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/list-memories.md): List memories for a user (or workspace).
- [Memory-backed passport feed: grouped doc rows + standalone memories.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/list-passport-feed.md): Single SQL UNION ALL: one row per documentId-with-memories (grouped on `raw_document_id`, joined to `raw_documents` for the status envelope) plus 1:1 standalone-memory rows (memories whose `raw_document_id IS NULL`).
- [Mark the document as extraction-failed.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/mark-extraction-failure.md): Service-owned status transition: callers declare *that* extraction failed and *what category* via a bounded `error_code`.
- [Mark the document as index-failed.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/mark-index-failure.md): Service-owned status transition.
- [OpenAPI Spec](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/openapi-spec.md): Download the canonical AtomicMemory Core OpenAPI document:
- [Register a pointer artifact or upload a managed artifact.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/put-storage-artifact.md): Pointer mode sends a JSON body with `mode: 'pointer'` plus a caller-supplied `uri`; the server stores the reference but NEVER fetches the URI.
- [Reconcile deferred mutations for a user (or all users when user_id is absent).](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/reconcile-deferred.md): Reconcile deferred mutations for a user (or all users when user_id is absent).
- [Register a pointer-only document.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/register-document.md): Idempotent on (user_id, source_site, provider, external_id, provider_version).
- [Report a new lesson.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/report-lesson.md): Report a new lesson.
- [Delete all memories for a given user + source_site.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/reset-by-source.md): Delete all memories for a given user + source_site.
- [Resolve a specific conflict with one of the three enum variants.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/resolve-agent-conflict.md): Resolve a specific conflict with one of the three enum variants.
- [Full semantic search with optional temporal / retrieval-mode / token-budget controls.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/search-memories.md): Full semantic search with optional temporal / retrieval-mode / token-budget controls.
- [Latency-optimized search (skips LLM repair loop). ~88% lower latency than /search.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/search-memories-fast.md): Latency-optimized search (skips LLM repair loop).
- [Set the calling user's trust level for a given agent.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/set-agent-trust.md): Set the calling user's trust level for a given agent.
- [Mutate runtime config (dev/test only). 410 when disabled.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/update-config.md): Set CORE_RUNTIME_CONFIG_MUTATION_ENABLED=true to enable.
- [Upload managed raw bytes for a registered document.](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/upload-raw-document.md): Stores the request body as the document's managed blob via the configured `RawContentStore` adapter (`local_fs` or `s3`), and promotes the document row to `storage_mode='managed_blob'` / `raw_storage_status='blob_stored'`.
- [Run the backend's verification (head-probe in v1).](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/api-reference/http/verify-storage-artifact.md): Pointer-mode artifacts always return `kind: 'unsupported'` — the server never fetches the registered URI.

## Optional

- [skill.md](https://docs.atomicstrata.ai/skill.md): Operating guide for agents reading these docs, when to read, how to navigate, citation conventions.

MCP descriptor: /.well-known/mcp.json (not a doc page, no link).
